material_language: warm archival paper with black ink saturation
compressed soil and mulch bed textures
lighting: clear daylight filtered through canopy, mixed with warm reading-room illumination across paper, soil, and translucent overlays
object_inventory: one broad cultivated commons field made from paper plots and soil seams
two large document-plate anchors carrying the civic and Black feminist archive weight
translucent support scaffolds crossing the field like grant rails
native-plant trial islands, root paths, and specimen apertures
small raster insets embedded as texture samples rather than main subjects
negative_constraints: no legible words, logos, interface labels, filenames, alphanumeric callouts, or readable document text
no desks, dashboards, software UI, card grid, archive wall, cabinet, shelves, or office-room staging
no glasshouse, conservatory, shrine, observatory, radio garden, misty listening field, or lounge atmosphere
do not let generic botanical wallpaper erase the civic media, Black feminist document, or funding infrastructure
do not make the grapefruit, forest, or dinosaur imagery the editorial thesis
no equal-weight grid of source objects
no literal depiction of the source reference image
